title: treasury / labor markets / competition | Economic Commentary | New Treasury Report Finds Corporate Concentration, Anti-comp...
summary: The U.S. Treasury, in coordination with DOJ, DOL, and FTC, released a report on March 7, 2022, finding that lack of competition in labor markets causes wage declines of roughly 20 percent for workers. The report identifies employer concentration, noncompete agreements, misclassification of employees as independent contractors, and declining unionization as key factors suppressing worker bargaining power. The Biden Administration announced these findings as part of Executive Order on Promoting Competition and outlined recommendations including increased antitrust enforcement, minimum wage increases, and easier worker organizing.
